stories.headline: Gov. Gavin Newsom Says Trump Is Investigating Him and His Wife
stories.summary: Aides to Gov. Gavin Newsom of California say several people associated with the couple have been contacted by federal agents in the past week. He criticized the move as politically motivated.

stories.headline: US fuel prices to take ‘months’ to normalise after US-Iran deal to end war
stories.summary: Producers will need time to ramp up output, while port bottlenecks and heightened demand will keep US prices up.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump says MoU with Tehran signed electronically
stories.summary: Backlash to US-Iran deal grows in Israel as Iranian vessels pass through Strait of Hormuz after US lifts naval blockade.

stories.headline: Bank of Japan raises rates to 1% for first time since 1995
stories.summary: Central bank deputy Shinichi Uchida to lead press conference after chief Kazuo Ueda hospitalised last week

stories.headline: 8 Dead in B-52 Bomber Crash at Edwards Air Force Base in California
stories.summary: The aircraft burst into flames on Monday morning shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base in California, officials said. The cause is still under investigation.

stories.headline: Netanyahu says Israel will remain in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza
stories.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says Israeli forces will maintain a ‘security zone’ in occupied Lebanon.

stories.headline: Japan raises interest rate to highest since 1995
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

stories.headline: Gavin Newsom says he’s being investigated by Trump
stories.summary: California’s Democratic Governor claims President Donald Trump has launched a criminal investigation against him.

stories.headline: BOJ hikes rates to highest level since 1995
Iran deal presents political nightmare for Netanyahu
stories.summary: Donald Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran leaves the Israeli PM trapped in a new political and security dilemma.

stories.headline: Sri Lanka sees ‘alarming’ rise in cybercrime as scam networks relocate from south-east Asia
stories.summary: Experts say criminal networks favour Sri Lanka due to ease of getting tourist visas and limited regulation on sim cards and internet connectionsExperts have warned that Sri Lanka is emerging as a hub for transnational cybercrime, after a crackdown in south-east Asia pushed Chinese-run criminal networks to relocate their vast scam operations.Sri Lankan police spokesperson Fredrick Wootler said the country was witnessing an “alarming increase of cy

stories.headline: US stock market climbs as US-Iran deal stirs hopes for end to energy chaos
stories.summary: Benchmark S&P 500 rises 1.7 percent, while tech-heavy Nasdaq jumps 3.1 percent.

stories.headline: China retail sales fall for first time since COVID lockdowns
Asian stocks leap, currencies rise as Trump says Hormuz will reopen
China’s economy shows signs of weakness in May
stories.summary: Retail sales decline for first time in more than three years and fixed asset investment slump deepens

stories.headline: The Two Clear Winners Are Iran and Markets
stories.summary: The deal hasn’t had much impact as yet on central banks and rate decisions.

stories.headline: Yen Pares Gains Versus Dollar After BOJ Hikes Key Rate to 1%
stories.summary: The yen pared gains against the dollar after the Bank of Japan raised its benchmark interest rate to the highest level since 1995 in a widely anticipated decision.

stories.headline: Middle East Oil Markets Weaken on Optimism Over Increased Supply
stories.summary: Middle Eastern crude markets weakened as the US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz spurred optimism around an increase in flows from the oil-rich region.

stories.headline: Evergrande Liquidators Seek Review of PwC HK’s $128 Million Deal
stories.summary: China Evergrande’s liquidators are seeking a judicial review of a HK$1 billion ($127.6 million) shareholder compensation deal a regulator struck with PricewaterhouseCoopers’ Hong Kong affiliate.

stories.headline: China’s Consumer Spending Drop Imperils Growth
stories.summary: China’s consumer spending contracted last month for the first time since the pandemic and investment deteriorated, exposing risks still facing the economy even as it benefits from booming exports and a deescalation in tensions around Iran. UBS Securities Chief China Economist Yu Song shares his analysis. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China’s Oil Refiners Slash Output After Crude Imports Plunge
stories.summary: Chinese oil refiners sharply reduced output last month to the weakest level in nearly four years, after the country’s crude imports plunged to an eight-year low due to the near-halt to shipments from the Persian Gulf.

stories.headline: Chinese Bonds Draw Foreign Funds in May After a Year of Outflows
stories.summary: Foreign investors returned to Chinese sovereign bonds in May for the first time in over a year, drawn by the market’s resilience while a brutal selloff battered global debt.

stories.headline: AllianzGI Trims Bullish Yuan Bet, Turns Neutral After Rally
stories.summary: Allianz Global Investors has reduced some of its bullish yuan position and turned neutral, taking profits after a rally that made the Chinese currency Asia’s best performer this year.

stories.headline: Worst Indonesian Credit Volatility in Years Imperils Debt Boom
stories.summary: The highest volatility in rupiah corporate bonds in four years is threatening to dent record issuance by companies in Southeast Asia’s largest economy at a time when government policy swings have hurt international investor confidence in Indonesian assets.

stories.headline: South Sudan’s Jonglei: Who burned homes and silenced hospitals?
stories.summary: Government and opposition forces accused of destruction in Jonglei amid burned villages and mass displacement.

stories.headline: US and Iran agree deal to end war as Trump says Strait of Hormuz to reopen
stories.summary: Both sides declare "the immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon", Pakistan says.

stories.headline: EU officially launches Ukraine and Moldova accession process
stories.summary: Kyiv sees EU accession as a security guarantee and alignment with Western values as it defends against Russian invasion.

stories.headline: Trump administration considers $300bn fund for Iran if deal is upheld
stories.summary: Incentives would be tied to Tehran’s ‘performance’ including over opening of the Strait of Hormuz and nuclear talks

stories.headline: Trump touts Iran deal and Ukraine ambition as he arrives at G7
stories.summary: Amid G7 tensions, Trump says Iran agreement is a step towards peace in the Middle East and beyond.

stories.headline: US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base
stories.summary: It was not immediately clear if there were any casualties in crash at base north of Los Angeles, California.

stories.headline: B-52 bomber crashes at Edwards Air Force Base in California
stories.summary: A B-52 Stratofortress went down at around 11:20 a.m. local time, with emergency crews scrambled to the scene. No casualty information has been released. The aircraft typically carries a crew of five.

stories.headline: Trump aims to end Iran war but nuclear issue remains unresolved
stories.summary: President Trump says the US and Iran have agreed a deal to end the war but it is unclear what has been agreed on Iran's nuclear programme

stories.headline: Average US petrol price falls below $4 on plan to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: As cost at the pump declines to nearly two-month low, analysts warn risk of a surge ‘has not completely disappeared’

stories.headline: Gavin Newsom says Trump has ordered justice department to investigate him
stories.summary: California governor is considered a potential Democratic presidential candidate for 2028

stories.headline: Bowen: Iran deal ends Trump's war that revealed limit of US dominance
stories.summary: The deal to end fighting and re-open the Strait of Hormuz leaves the sides where they were 24 hours before the war - only with thousands now dead.

stories.headline: Moscow strike on Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra 'one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture'
stories.summary: The Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra, one of the most important historic and religious symbols of Ukraine, has been hit in Moscow's drone and missile attack overnight on Monday, in what President Zelenskyy has called “one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture to date.”

stories.headline: Starmer vows new sanctions on Russia and nuclear energy support for Ukraine
stories.summary: G7 told ‘we will stand with Ukraine for as long as it takes’, with Russia’s finance networks and shadow fleet targetedKeir Starmer has vowed to “choke off” Russian revenue with further sanctions and to provide hundreds of millions of pounds worth of energy support for Ukraine, as he met world leaders in France for the G7.After a torrid political week at home, the British prime minister sought to put himself on the front foot on the international

stories.headline: California Governor Newsom says US Department of Justice investigating him
stories.summary: Newsom says he and wife targeted because he is considering running for president in 2028; DOJ has not confirmed probe.

stories.headline: 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies falling into volcanic crater
stories.summary: Al-Qaqa Ibn Antar had been attempting to climb its walls without safety equipment when he fell in, local authorities say.

stories.headline: Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
stories.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people.

stories.headline: Unclear if US-Iran agreement will provide respite to Lebanon
stories.summary: Pakistan says the deal includes Lebanon, but recent ceasefires there have failed to take hold.

stories.headline: Political tension has undermined World Cup joy, says Iran striker
stories.summary: Iran's striker Mehdi Taremi said the country's political predicament "undermines the joy of the World Cup".

stories.headline: Lawmakers Warn Trump Officials Not to Pursue Arch Project Without Congress
stories.summary: In a letter to Interior Secretary Doug Burgum and national parks officials, several Democrats and a Senate independent said that members of the administration could face fines and even criminal prosecution.

stories.headline: A Bystander at a ‘No Kings’ March Was Killed. Did the Shooter Commit a Crime?
stories.summary: A gunman believed he was defending the crowd, then killed an innocent protester. The legal case is complicated.

stories.headline: One Nation branch official defended Hitler Youth and called Aboriginal people ‘stone age’ in racist posts
stories.summary: Exclusive: Aboriginal Australians and migrants the targets of John Drew, who claims he was once kicked out of Pauline Hanson’s party for being ‘too radical’ – but is now a branch officialWarning: This article contains racially offensive language and outdated referencesFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA man who has defended the Hitler Youth organisation and used offens

stories.headline: 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies after fall into volcanic crater
stories.summary: A Yemeni adventurer known online as the “Spider-Man of Yemen” died after falling into a volcanic crater while attempting a climb without safety equipment.

stories.headline: South Korea's Starbucks to shut for staff history lesson after backlash
stories.summary: The chain was criticised for a "Tank Day" marketing ploy on the anniversary of a military massacre.

stories.headline: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.

stories.headline: Oil Heads for Longest Losing Run This Year Following Hormuz Deal
stories.summary: Oil headed for its longest losing streak this year as the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosted expectations for a revival in supply, with leading Wall Street banks reducing their price forecasts.

stories.headline: Asia Stock Rally Falters, BOJ Hikes as Expected: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian stocks wavered as investors paused to assess the durability of the relief rally fueled by the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The Bank of Japan raised its benchmark rate as expected to the highest level since 1995.

stories.headline: SpaceX Surges for 2nd Day to Add $412 Billion in Value
stories.summary: SpaceX shares jumped in their second day of trading, adding to gains following a blockbuster debut that instantly vaulted it into the ranks of the world’s most valuable public companies.

stories.headline: Trader Loses $1 Million on Cabo Verde’s Stunning World Cup Tie
stories.summary: A single trader on Polymarket lost nearly $1 million when Cabo Verde fought Spain to a stunning draw on Monday in one of the most unlikely outcomes in recent World Cup history, powered by a 40-year-old goalkeeper who left the pitch in tears.

stories.headline: China Built the Last Steel Boom. India’s Will Be Different
stories.summary: The next big growth market will align closely with domestic spending on infrastructure.

stories.headline: Bolivia Tells Investors FX Unification, IMF Deal Coming Soon
stories.summary: Bolivia told investors it is close to agreeing on a financing program with the International Monetary Fund, likely after introducing a floating exchange rate to end a dollar peg that has been in place for more than 15 years.

stories.headline: Dan Sullivan Namesake Is Disqualified From Alaska Senate Ballot
stories.summary: Republicans had accused Democrats of attempting to confuse voters by boosting a candidate with the same name as the incumbent Republican senator.

stories.headline: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies
stories.summary: Outpouring of public grief for Lidia ‘Taty’ Almeida, leader of group of mothers that has marched every week since 1977The human rights activist Lidia “Taty” Almeida – who spent more than half a century searching for her son after he was forcibly disappeared by Argentina’s military junta – has died aged 95, prompting a public outpouring of grief.Almeida, 95, was the president of the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo, made up of women who have marched aroun

stories.headline: Italian police dismantle illicit bank used by drug traffickers as global broker
stories.summary: Clandestine operation run by Chinese national in Prato moved €80-100m a year through intermediariesItalian police have dismantled an underground bank used by drug traffickers through which several hundred million euros are believed to have moved over at least three years.The clandestine bank, whose logistical base was located in Prato, north-west of Florence, has been run since 2021 by a Chinese national, officials said. Continue reading...

stories.headline: US judge dismisses Musk’s xAI trade secret lawsuit against OpenAI
stories.summary: The lawsuit originally filed in September focused on broader alleged misappropriation of confidential information.

stories.headline: EU fails to reach agreement on sanctioning Israel's Ben Gvir despite member state pressure
stories.summary: The bloc's foreign policy chief confirmed that discussions on restricting trade with illegal West Bank settlements will also continue ahead of July's ministerial meeting.

stories.headline: Two men convicted of arson plot targeting UK prime minister's properties
stories.summary: Two men face sentencing Friday after being found guilty of torching properties linked to the prime minister, in a case that has raised questions about foreign interference in British public life.

stories.headline: Czech government scraps licence fees for public media in move critics call threat to press freedom
stories.summary: The right-wing populist coalition of Prime Minister Andrej Babiš has approved a bill replacing the traditional funding model with direct state financing, triggering strikes and protests.

stories.headline: Spanish police conclude initials 'P.S.' in diary of former party member refer to PM Pedro Sánchez
stories.summary: A Civil Guard report identifies the initials "P.S." with the prime minister and names Santos Cerdán as the driving force behind efforts to derail the case against Sánchez's brother.

stories.headline: Do the World Cup's pricey tickets reflect US market rates and stop scalpers, as FIFA says?
stories.summary: FIFA says World Cup ticket prices reflect US market rates and help prevent scalpers from profiting. But experts and consumer groups say the comparison with US sports leaves out important context.

stories.headline: Israeli military says Hezbollah targets struck in Beirut
stories.summary: Israel says it targeted the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Tehran warns this could derail a US-Iran deal to end the fighting.

stories.headline: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike, Trump says
stories.summary: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
stories.summary: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

stories.headline: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
stories.summary: Japanese firms will spend billions on UK infrastructure and offshore wind, Downing Street says.

stories.headline: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
stories.summary: The highly controversial trend lies at the intersection of Russia's war on Ukraine, new AI technologies and grief.

stories.headline: Vincent's parents 'never say he's good enough' - so he turned to a middle-aged couple online
stories.summary: A niche group of content creators called "virtual parents" have become hugely popular among young people.

stories.headline: Jessie J’s triumphant return puts lucrative Chinese market in spotlight

stories.headline: The college scam that promised students fleeing war a new life in Finland
stories.summary: BBC speaks to students from war-torn Myanmar who say they were cheated after paying to study abroad.

stories.headline: Indonesian students protest against state spending, fuel price hike
stories.summary: The protesters warn wasteful government spending risks driving the country into bankruptcy.

stories.headline: Israeli tech firm accused of targeting First Minister in election
stories.summary: Accounts linked to tech firm BlackCore are alleged to have carried out 'digital interference operations' against John Swinney.

stories.headline: Jailed South Korea ex-president gets 30 more years for sending drones into North
stories.summary: Prosecutors argued that Yoon ordered the operation to provoke Pyongyang and create a pretext for martial law.

stories.headline: Watch: Fans celebrate Mexico's win in World Cup opening match
stories.summary: There were wild celebrations in host nation Mexico, as their team kicked off their World Cup campaign with a 2-0 win over South Africa.

stories.headline: SpaceX IPO in Focus as Iran Fears Ease | The Asia Trade 6/12/2026
stories.summary:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: The value of SpaceX rockets on its stock-market debut
stories.summary: Elon Musk has become the world’s first trillionaire

stories.headline: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira, dancing, and protests
stories.summary: There was a star-studded opening ceremony, and some sporadic clashes between police and protesters around the Azteca stadium.

stories.headline: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
